diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever with rigors is one limb of Charcot's triad and a hallmark of bacteraemic biliary infection.
Second limb of Charcot's triad; reflects biliary tract inflammation/obstruction.
RUQ pain is shared with gallbladder pathology; imaging distinguishes them.

symptoms.symptom_name: Jaundice — yellowing of eyes and skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Third limb of Charcot's triad; indicates biliary obstruction with the infective process.
Jaundice is not a feature of a colonic flare; it localises the problem to the biliary tree/liver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Scleral icterus / visible jaundice on video

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Video-observable and caregiver-reported yellowing is patient-obtainable in telehealth (unverified); corroborates cholestasis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Month of progressive fatigue, pruritus, fluctuating jaundice, weight loss and steatorrhoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic cholestatic syndrome — the substrate for recurrent cholangitis; weight loss also flags a cholangiocarcinoma concern.
PSC is strongly associated with inflammatory bowel disease, especially ulcerative colitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension, tachycardia and tachypnoea (BP 95/60, HR 122, RR 24)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective sepsis physiology — clinician-measured and sealed; the AI infers systemic compromise from rigors and the patient feeling toxic, not from a measured blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender palpable liver edge (hepatomegaly) and mild splenomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender hepatomegaly supports hepatobiliary inflammation; requires palpation the AI cannot perform — sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods — FBC, LFTs (cholestatic), CRP, lactate, blood cultures

investigation_details.investigation_name: Septic screen and liver function tests
investigation_details.expected_result: Neutrophilia, raised CRP, cholestatic LFT pattern, raised bilirubin, possible raised lactate, positive blood cultures
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Sepsis severity and biliary obstruction go unquantified; delays antibiotics and drainage.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms systemic inflammation and cholestasis; unavailable in telehealth in real time.

symptoms.symptom_name: MRCP showing beaded, multifocally strictured bile ducts

investigation_details.expected_result: Multifocal biliary strictures with intervening dilatation ('beaded' ducts) characteristic of PSC
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Underlying stricturing disease and the level of obstruction remain unconfirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Beaded bile ducts on MRCP are the classic imaging signature of PSC — a hospital investigation, never available remotely.
Patient-obtainable home temperature (unverified) corroborates a high fever; stored as the patient-stated string, never parsed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Fever/rigors + RUQ pain + jaundice together are the classic triad of acute cholangitis and mandate urgent escalation.
Progressive cholestatic symptoms in a patient with ulcerative colitis point to underlying stricturing biliary disease as the substrate for recurrent cholangitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency bloods — FBC, LFTs, CRP, lactate, coagulation, blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies sepsis severity and cholestasis to guide antibiotics and drainage — obtained in hospital, not telehealth.
Identifies biliary obstruction/strictures and the need for decompression; MRCP characterises PSC.
